title: Remote Module Fetches
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM FetchRemoteModule WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(Module_Localised, Module) AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM FetchRemoteModule WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Module ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {total} module(s) retrieved from remote storage during the report period. Fetching modules remotely saves a cross-bubble trip but adds a transfer surcharge — a high fetch count shows active multi-ship fleet management and suggests you're keeping several builds operational simultaneously across different locations.
details.help: FetchRemoteModule Module_Localised is the module type retrieved. total drives the title.

tagLines: Remote module retrieval log compiled. Fleet storage access events noted in the outfitting record.
The module was in storage somewhere inconvenient. You paid to have it moved rather than fly for it.
Remote fetching is the lazy engineer's convenience fee. Lazy engineers keep more ships operational.
